title: Transaction manager with MVCC, locking, SSI, and savepoints
description: Implement Transaction struct: txn_id, status (Active/Committed/Aborted), isolation_level, snapshot (xmin/xmax/active_txns), write_set, read_set, savepoints, timestamps. Implement MVCC visibility rules: tuple visible if xmin committed+in-snapshot AND (xmax invalid OR xmax aborted OR xmax not-in-snapshot), with special handling for own-transaction writes. Implement Snapshot creation per isolation level: READ COMMITTED (new per statement), REPEATABLE READ (one per txn), SERIALIZABLE (one per txn + rw tracking). Implement lock manager: row-level Shared/Exclusive/SIRead locks, HashMap<LockTarget,LockEntry>, FIFO wait queue, intention locks for DDL. Implement deadlock detection: wait-for graph with DFS cycle detection every 100ms, abort victim with fewest writes. Implement SSI: SIREAD locks (advisory, never block), rw-dependency tracking, dangerous structure detection (T1→rw→T2→rw→T3 where T1,T3 concurrent), abort on commit. Implement savepoints: stack of (name, write_set_position, lock_count), ROLLBACK TO undoes writes after position, RELEASE discards marker. Include tests for all visibility rules, snapshot isolation, write-write conflicts, deadlock scenarios, SSI write-skew prevention, savepoint nesting.
result_summary:
All tests pass. The entire transaction manager system is already fully implemented:

**Source files** (all in `sqld/src/transaction/`):
- `mod.rs` - Module exports with all public types
- `transaction.rs` - `Transaction` struct (txn_id, status, isolation_level, snapshot, write_set, read_set, savepoints, timestamps, command_id) + `TransactionManager` (atomic ID generation, begin/commit/abort, snapshot creation, refresh)
- `mvcc.rs` - `Snapshot` (xmin/xmax/active_txns), `TxnStatusLookup` trait, `VisibilityCheck` (PostgreSQL-style visibility rules, write-write conflict detection)
- `lock_manager.rs` - Row-level Shared/Exclusive/SIRead/IntentionShared/IntentionExclusive locks, FIFO wait queue, lock upgrade, deadlock detection via DFS cycle detection on wait-for graph
- `ssi.rs` - `SsiManager` with rw-dependency tracking, dangerous structure detection (T1->rw->T2->rw->T3 where T1||T3), 2-txn write-skew detection, abort handling
- `savepoint.rs` - `Savepoint` struct (name, write_set_position, read_set_position)

**Test files** (all in `sqld/tests/`):
- `test_mvcc_visibility.rs` - 28 tests covering all visibility rules, write conflicts
- `test_snapshot.rs` - 35 tests for snapshot creation per isolation level
- `test_transactions.rs` - 30 tests for transaction lifecycle
- `test_isolation_read_committed.rs` - 12 tests for READ COMMITTED (per-statement refresh)
- `test_isolation_snapshot.rs` - 11 tests for REPEATABLE READ
- `test_isolation_serializable.rs` - 15 tests for SSI integration
- `test_lock_manager.rs` - 80 tests for lock compatibility, upgrades, FIFO, SIRead
- `test_deadlock.rs` - 47 tests for deadlock detection/resolution
- `test_savepoints.rs` - 43 tests for savepoint nesting, rollback, release

**Result: 0 failures across all test suites.** The implementation is complete and all tests pass.
